title: werauhia clandestina
intro: Werauhia clandestina, also known as the 'Ghost Fern', is a rare and unique species of fern native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Werauhia clandestina is often used in infusions, decoctions, or creams, and is believed to be most effective when combined with other plants for enhanced medicinal properties.
